commit.message:
fix: enforce per-unit weight/dimension convention across vision pipeline

Weight, dimensions, and volume are now always estimated for ONE unit.
Quantity is the multiplier; downstream code computes totals via
weight_lbs * quantity. This prevents inflated totals when grouped items
(e.g., "100 books at 100 lbs") were ambiguously treated as total vs
per-unit weight.

Changes:
- itemEstimationService: prompt explicitly requests per-unit estimates,
  quantity removed from AI context to prevent confusion
- geminiVideoScanService: prompt uses singular unit names with quantity
  field instead of baking count into item name
- visionService: photo prompt returns quantity field, uses singular names
- censusAgentService: tool descriptions and system prompt clarify
  per-unit convention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- 411 camps: city case normalization (TORONTO/toronto → Toronto)
- 130 camps: city typo (MIssissauga → Mississauga)
- 1 camp: city typo (Toront0 → Toronto)
- 2 camps: activated (had active programs but status=0)

fix: release all stuck keys after taskkill to prevent Windows input issues

"pressed" in Windows causing broken mouse wheel, Firefox and Discord.
Added releaseAllKeys() that sends key-up events for all hybrid keys
after process termination.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): expand minor accordion before checking finding visibility

When ALL findings are minor severity, they're hidden in the collapsed
accordion (Story 4.1a progressive disclosure). The E2E tests must
expand the accordion BEFORE asserting finding-compact-row visibility.

Previous fix had accordion expansion after the visibility check,
causing failures when the pipeline only produced minor findings.

commit.message:
fix: prevent duplicate stopSpinner from erasing AI response after tool calls

The safety stopSpinner() after runner.run() was unconditionally clearing
the terminal line, erasing the AI's final text response that had already
been rendered by the onToken callback. Guard it with streamingStarted so
it only fires when no response was streamed.

commit.message:
Dynamic newspaper layout: rotate article placement around editorial sections

Each thematic section now cycles through 5 layout variants to eliminate
white space and give a genuine newspaper feel:

- right: prose left, article cards stacked on right
- left: cards on left, prose on right
- top: articles in horizontal row above full-width 2-col prose
- bottom: full-width prose above, articles in horizontal row below
- surround: articles flanking prose on both sides (CSS grid 3-col)
- wide: 2-column prose when section has no cited articles

Layout is selected by rotating _LAYOUT_SEQ by section index, adjusted for
article count (single articles alternate left/right; surround needs 2+).

Row-mode cards (top/bottom) use a shorter thumbnail and 2-line lede.
Responsive: surround collapses to single column, row cards wrap.
Print: surround grid uses fixed pt widths, prose-wide goes 2-col.

commit.message:
fix(ml): preserve else-branch defaults when no original feature_context

The previous commit injected historicalOccupancy into feature_context
even when the caller passed None, causing add_bridge_day_feature to run
and crash with KeyError('date_local') — that function was never called
in the no-context path before.

Fix: only inject into feature_context when it was non-None. In the else
branch, call add_park_occupancy_feature directly with a minimal context
containing only historicalOccupancy + baseTime, leaving all other
defaults (bridge_day=0, virtual_queue=0, etc.) unchanged.

fix: force setActive() in OrgGate when org is present but missing from JWT

When a user signs in, Clerk knows the active org client-side but the JWT
cookie may not include orgId yet. OrgGate now calls setActive() to write
the org into the JWT, ensuring server-side auth().orgId is populated
before any API fetches fire.

  fetch failures don't break the entire refresh cycle
- Unstake now pre-checks cooldown + permanent balance on-chain before
  sending the tx, returning a specific reason string on failure
- Unstake automatically caps to unlocked amount (excludes permanent)
- Forge page shows the exact cooldown reason from the contract

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE for real content

When all detectors return 0 (no AI signals found), combinedAiScore=0 was
being passed as undefined, which defaulted to 50 in the engine (grey zone)
and landed in INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.

Fix: always pass the combined score. Zero = no AI evidence = Likely Original.
Also change engine fallback from 50 to 0 for the same reason.
